questionList.choiceQuestionContent: During an evacuation drill, people leave a building at a rate of R t( ) people per minute, where t is the number of minutes since the start of the drill. Selected values of R t( ) are shown in the table above. Using a right Riemann sum with three subintervals and data from the table, what is the approximation of the number of people who leave the building during the first 15 minutes of the evacuation drill?

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Let g be a twice-differentiable, increasing function of t. If g(0) = 20 and g(10) = 220, which of the following must be true on the interval 0 < t < 10?

optionList.optionContent: g'(t) = 0 for some t in the interval.
g'(t) = 20 for some t in the interval.
g"(t) = 0 for some t in the interval.
g"(t) > 0 for all t in the interval.

questionOption:
A .

g'(t) = 0 for some t in the interval.

B .

g'(t) = 20 for some t in the interval.

C .

g"(t) = 0 for some t in the interval.

D .

g"(t) > 0 for all t in the interval.

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: The table above gives values of the continuous function f at selected values of x. If f has exactly two critical points on the open interval (10, 14), which of the following must be true?

optionList.optionContent: f(x) > 0 for all x in the open interval (10, 14).
f'(x) exists for all x in the open interval (10, 14 ).
f'(x) < 0 for all x in the open interval (10, 11).
f'(12) ≠ 0

questionOption:
A .

f(x) > 0 for all x in the open interval (10, 14).

B .

f'(x) exists for all x in the open interval (10, 14 ).

C .

f'(x) < 0 for all x in the open interval (10, 11).

questionList.questionTitle: Meaning of derivative with water leak
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: The rate at which water leaks from a tank, in gallons per hour, is modeled by R, a differentiable function of the number of hours after the leak is discovered. Which of the following is the best interpretation of R'(3)?

optionList.optionContent: The amount of water, in gallons, that has leaked out of the tank during the first three hours after the leak is discovered
The amount of change, in gallons per hour, in the rate at which water is leaking during the three hours after the leak is discovered
The rate at which water leaks from the tank, in gallons per hour, three hours after the leak is discovered
The rate of change of the rate at which water leaks from the tank, in gallons per hour per hour, three hours after the leak is discovered

questionOption:
A .

The amount of water, in gallons, that has leaked out of the tank during the first three hours after the leak is discovered

B .

The amount of change, in gallons per hour, in the rate at which water is leaking during the three hours after the leak is discovered

C .

The rate at which water leaks from the tank, in gallons per hour, three hours after the leak is discovered

D .

The rate of change of the rate at which water leaks from the tank, in gallons per hour per hour, three hours after the leak is discovered

questionList.questionTitle: Grain silo: Approx., model accum., rate behavior

subjectiveQuestionList.questionContent:
CALCULUS BC

SECTION II, Part A

Time - 30 minutes

Number of questions - 2

A GRAPHING CALCULATOR IS REQUIRED FOR THESE QUESTIONS.

1. Grain is being added to a silo. At time t=0 MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaagKart1ev2aaatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaGaamiDaiabg2 da9iaaicdaaaa@38AF@ , the silo is empty. The rate at which grain is being added is modeled by the differentiable function g, where g(t) MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaagKart1ev2aaatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaGaam4zaiaacI cacaWG0bGaaiykaaaa@3934@  is measured in cubic feet per minute for 0≤t≤8 MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaagKart1ev2aaatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaaeaaaaaaaaa8 qacaaIWaGaeyizImQaamiDaiabgsMiJkaaiIdaaaa@3BF5@  minutes. Selected values of g(t) MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaagKart1ev2aaatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaGaam4zaiaacI cacaWG0bGaaiykaaaa@3934@  are given in the table above.

(a) Using the data in the table, approximate g ' (3) MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaagKart1ev2aaatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaGaam4zamaaCa aaleqabaGaai4jaaaakiaacIcacaaIZaGaaiykaaaa@39DA@ . Using correct units, interpret the meaning of g ' (3) MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaagKart1ev2aaatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaGaam4zamaaCa aaleqabaGaai4jaaaakiaacIcacaaIZaGaaiykaaaa@39DA@  in the context of the problem.

(b) Write an integral expression that represents the total amount of grain added to the silo from time  t=0 MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaagKart1ev2aaatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaaeaaaaaaaaa8 qacaWG0bGaeyypa0JaaGimaaaa@38CF@ to time t=8 MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaagKart1ev2aaatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaaeaaaaaaaaa8 qacaWG0bGaeyypa0JaaGimaaaa@38CF@ . Use a right Riemann sum with the four subintervals indicated by the data in the table to approximate the integral.

(c) The grain in the silo is spoiling at a rate modeled by w(t)=32⋅sin(πt74)MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaahmart1ev3aaatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaaeaaaaaaaaa8 qacaWG3bGaaiikaiaadshacaGGPaGaeyypa0JaaG4maiaaikdacqGH flY1daGcaaWdaeaapeGaci4CaiaacMgacaGGUbWaaeWaa8aabaWdbm aalaaapaqaa8qacqaHapaCcaWG0baapaqaa8qacaaI3aGaaGinaaaa aiaawIcacaGLPaaaaSqabaaaaa@4766@ , where w(t) MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaagKart1ev2aaatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaaeaaaaaaaaa8 qacaWG3bWdaiaacIcapeGaamiDa8aacaGGPaaaaa@3992@ is measured in cubic feet per minute for  0 ≤ t ≤ 8 MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaagKart1ev2aaatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaaeaaaaaaaaa8 qacaaIWaGaeyizImQaamiDaiabgsMiJkaaiIdaaaa@3BF5@  minutes. Using the result from part (b), approximate the amount of unspoiled grain remaining in the silo at time t = 8 MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaagKart1ev2aaatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaaeaaaaaaaaa8 qacaWG0bGaeyypa0JaaGimaaaa@38CF@ .

(d) Based on the model in part (c), is the amount of unspoiled grain in the silo increasing or decreasing at time t = 6 MathType@MTEF@5@5@+= feaagKart1ev2aaatCvAUfeBSjuyZL2yd9gzLbvyNv2CaerbuLwBLn hiov2DGi1BTfMBaeXatLxBI9gBaerbd9wDYLwzYbItLDharqqtubsr 4rNCHbGeaGqiVu0Je9sqqrpepC0xbbL8F4rqqrFfpeea0xe9Lq=Jc9 vqaqpepm0xbba9pwe9Q8fs0=yqaqpepae9pg0FirpepeKkFr0xfr=x fr=xb9adbaqaaeGaciGaaiaabeqaamaabaabaaGcbaaeaaaaaaaaa8 qacaWG0bGaeyypa0JaaGimaaaa@38CF@ ? 3. partList.directionsText:
CALCULUS BC
SECTION I, Part A
Time - 1 hour
30 Questions

 

NO CALCULATOR IS ALLOWED FOR THIS PART OF THE EXAM.

 

Directions: Solve each of the following problems, using the available space for scratch work. After examining the form of the choices, decide which is the best of the choices given and fill in the corresponding circle on the answer sheet. No credit will be given for anything written in this examn booklet. Do not spend too much time on any one problem.

 

In this exam:

1.Unless otherwise specified, the domain of a function f is assumed to be the set of all real numbers x for which f(x) is a real number.

2.The inverse of a trigonometric function f may be indicated using the inverse function notation f⁻¹ or with the prefix “arc” (e.g.,sin⁻¹ x= arcsin x).

CALCULUS BC
SECTION I, Part B
Time - 45 minutes
15 Questions

 

A GRAPHING CALCULATOR IS REQUIRED FOR SOME QUESTIONS ON THIS PART OF THE EXAM.

 

Directions: Solve each of the following problems, using the available space for scratch work. After examining the form of the choices, decide which is the best of the choices given and fill in the corresponding circle on the answer sheet. No credit will be given for anything written in this exam booklet. Do not spend too much time on any one problem.

 

BE SURE YOU FILL IN THE CIRCLES ON THE ANSWER SHEET THAT CORRESPOND TO QUESTIONS NUMBERED 76-90.
YOU MAY NOT RETURN TO QUESTIONS NUMBERED 1-30.

 

In this exam:

1. The exact numerical value of the correct answer does not always appear among the choices given. When this happens, select from among the choices the number that best approximates the exact numerical value.

2.Unless otherwise specified, the domain of a function f is assumed to be the set of all real numbers x for which f(x) is a real number.

3.The inverse of a trigonometric function f may be indicated using the inverse function notation f⁻¹ or with the prefix “arc” (e.g.,sin⁻¹ x= arcsin x).

CALCULUS BC
SECTION II, Part A
Time - 30 minutes
2 Questions


A GRAPHING CALCULATOR IS REQUIRED FOR THESE QUESTIONS.

CALCULUS BC
SECTION Ⅱ, Part B
Time - 1 hour
4 Questions


NO CALCULATOR IS ALLOWED FOR THESE QUESTIONS.


DO NOT BREAK THE SEALS UNTIL YOU ARE TOLD TO DO SO.
